label: PMID_36449351
title: Heimao tea polysaccharides ameliorate obesity by enhancing gut microbiota-dependent adipocytes thermogenesis in mice fed with high fat diet

label: PMID_36449351_R1
claim_text_raw: Heimao tea polysaccharides ameliorated obesity by enhancing gut microbiota-dependent adipocyte thermogenesis in mice fed a high-fat diet, with Dubosiella and Romboutsia closely associated with the anti-obesity effect.

label: PMID_36449351_R2
claim_text_raw: Dubosiella and Romboutsia increased significantly and were negatively correlated with body weight and positively correlated with iBAT index in HMTP-treated obese mice.

label: PMID_39479919
title: Inhibitory effect of tea flower polysaccharides on oxidative stress and microglial oxidative damage in aging mice by regulating gut microbiota

label: PMID_39479919_R1
claim_text_raw: TFPS improved gut microbiota homeostasis, reduced the Firmicutes to Bacteroidota ratio, increased Lactobacillus, increased SOD CAT and POD activity, and reduced MDA in aging mice.

label: PMID_39479919_R2
claim_text_raw: TFPS reduced excessive hippocampal microglial activation, down-regulated IL-6 IL-1beta TNF-alpha and NF-kB, and alleviated neuroinflammation in aging mice while regulating gut microbiota.

label: PMID_39574401
title: Tea Polyphenols Reduced Obesity by Modulating Gut Microbiota-SCFAs-Barrier and Inflammation in High-Fat Diet-Induced Mice

label: PMID_39574401_R1
claim_text_raw: Tea polyphenols attenuated HFD-induced weight gain, liver and adipose accumulation, improved liver fat content, and suppressed inflammatory signaling while modulating gut microbiota and SCFAs.

claim_text_raw: Tea polyphenols increased Blautia and Faecalibaculum, reduced Colidextribacter, activated G protein-coupled receptors, inhibited histone deacetylases, enhanced intestinal tight junction expression, and preserved intestinal barrier integrity.

claim_text_raw: Regular black tea kombucha consumption positively influenced gut microbiota in both normal and obese groups, with enrichment of commensal bacteria such as Bacteroidota and Akkermansiaceae and reduction of Ruminococcus and Dorea, especially in the obese group.

label: PMID_40957830
journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry
title: Bai Mao Tea Polysaccharides Induce Adipose Tissue Browning and Ameliorate Diet-Induced Obesity by Modulating Butyrate Production and Gut Microbiota Composition

label: PMID_40957830_R1
claim_text_raw: BMTP significantly reduced body weight, improved insulin sensitivity, decreased adipocyte volume, increased multivesicular adipocyte-like cells, and upregulated UCP1 in iWAT and iBAT.
